mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales indicate robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as market participants price in future growth.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth does not correlate positively with S&P 500 20-day returns over multiple business cycles, or if the correlation turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as economic concerns mount.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive or flat following a significant rise in UNRATE in more than 40% of triggered instances during economic contractions.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with rising interest rates indicates aggressive monetary tightening, which increases the cost of capital and reduces future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in 1 month
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both inflation is high and FEDFUNDS is actively rising, or if the signal's predictive power is negligible.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, positive surprises in manufacturing data tend to boost market sentiment, leading to short-term positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth surprises do not lead to positive S&P 500 5-day returns during periods of confirmed economic expansion, or if returns are consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening future construction activity and broader economic demand, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 performance over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average falls > 10% from prior 3-month average
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive or flat following significant and sustained declines in housing starts in more than 35% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Robust non-farm payroll growth indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenues, which generally leads to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If strong PAYEMS growth does not consistently precede positive S&P 500 20-day returns during periods of economic expansion, or if the relationship reverses.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate, especially after a period of tightening, signals an accommodative monetary policy stance, which supports asset valuations by lowering discount rates and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat during extended periods of low and stable FEDFUNDS rates in more than 30% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with robust industrial production indicates broad-based economic strength, signaling healthy corporate earnings and positive short-term market sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annual growth > 2% AND INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.3%
failure_condition: If periods of simultaneous strong GDP and industrial production growth do not lead to positive S&P 500 5-day returns, or if the combined signal shows no predictive power.
